title: 'Texas Chainsaw Massacre' and the Most Extreme, Disreputable Movies Ever
format: Netflix's tenth 'Texas Chainsaw Massacre' is the excuse. The real subject is fifty years of disreputable cinema, and Alex Ross Perry brought a spreadsheet.

segments.heading: 'Texas Chainsaw Massacre' (2022) and the Ten-Movie Franchise
segments.note: a street sequel, and a head-spinning attempt to map the timeline

films.title: Texas Chainsaw Massacre
films.note: Netflix's direct sequel to the 1974 original. Mixed bag: half social commentary, half kill fest, doesn't quite work, but the final 90 seconds land. 74 minutes long, a 31% Tomatometer score, and a Sally Hardesty legacy-sequel angle that gets almost no screen time.

films.title: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre
films.note: The original, in Perry's top 10 of all time. 16mm poetry, palpably gross production design, a family dynamic nothing else in the franchise matches.

films.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre 2
films.note: Tobe Hooper's own sequel, and Fennessey's favorite of the sequels: a comedy where the original is grindhouse horror, written by L.M. Kit Carson.

films.title: Leatherface
films.note: Leatherface: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre III, New Line's entry in the original four-film timeline.

films.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Next Generation
films.note: closes out the original Tobe Hooper lineage's timeline

films.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre
films.note: the Platinum Dunes remake

films.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Beginning
films.note: Platinum Dunes' prequel to their own remake

films.title: Texas Chainsaw 3D
films.note: a direct sequel to the 1974 original that ignores every other sequel

films.title: Leatherface
films.note: Perry's easy pick for second-best in the franchise: a prequel from the directors of 'Inside,' with a French New Extremity nastiness and real sympathy for how the boy becomes the monster.

films.title: Inside
films.note: the French horror film by the directors of 2017's 'Leatherface'

films.title: X
films.note: Ti West's upcoming release, teased as delivering what this movie doesn't

films.title: Scream
films.note: cited as a legacy sequel that actually balances new characters against old ones

films.title: Halloween
films.note: the comparison point for resurrecting an aging slasher IP

films.title: Evil Dead
films.note: producer Fede Alvarez's prior remake

films.title: Malignant
films.note: Perry's example of a film that needed a rewatch to click

segments.heading: The Sex Lane: Nudie Cuties to Deep Throat
segments.note: extreme sexuality and extreme violence as the two ways of getting people out of the house

films.title: Mom and Dad
films.note: the exploitation 'hygiene film' Perry points to as the starting line for the genre

films.title: Summer with Monika
films.note: a Bergman film plenty of people went to see for the nudity

films.title: I Am Curious Yellow
films.note: sold as art, watched as something closer to pornography

films.title: Deep Throat
films.note: the mainstream crossover into true pornography

films.title: Ilsa, She Wolf of the SS
films.note: cited as the deeper end of the sexploitation cycle

segments.heading: The Violent Lane: Grindhouse to Tarantino
segments.note: from post-Vietnam shock to the tape-trading 90s

films.title: The Wild Bunch
films.note: a studio-funded outlier ahead of the 1970s curve

films.title: A Clockwork Orange
films.note: the other studio-funded outlier

films.title: The Last House on the Left
films.note: Wes Craven's debut: a Bergman revenge story dragged through the American gutter

films.title: The Exorcist
films.note: the one film on the list that also crosses over into official 1970s Hollywood history

films.title: I Spit on Your Grave
films.note: described as dirtier under the fingernails than its peers

films.title: The Beyond
films.note: Lucio Fulci, reclaimed in the late 90s: genuinely difficult to watch and phantasmagoric

films.title: Faces of Death
films.note: the quasi-documentary compilation series now valuable enough as IP that Legendary is reportedly developing a new one

films.title: Bumfights: Cause for Concern
films.note: sold direct-to-VHS via cable ad, the era's other tape-trading curio

films.title: Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer
films.note: low-budget indie, paired against 'Natural Born Killers' as the studio-scale version of the same 90s cycle

films.title: Reservoir Dogs
films.note: Tarantino's taste, more than any single scene, is credited with widening what American audiences would sit through

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: Entertainment Weekly's editor-in-chief reportedly called it a mainstreaming of sadism

films.title: The Killing of America
films.note: a Japanese documentary partly credited to Leonard Schrader (Paul Schrader's brother), billed as 'before Bowling for Columbine'

films.title: Mondo Cane
films.note: the shockumentary that somehow landed an Oscar nomination for its score

segments.heading: Torture Porn and the French New Extremity

films.title: High Tension
films.note: called an absolute masterpiece of the French movement that also produced 2017's 'Leatherface'

films.title: Audition
films.note: one of the Takashi Miike films cited for brutality

films.title: Oldboy
films.note: part of the Korean and Hong Kong wave Tarantino's taste helped popularize stateside

segments.heading: Found Footage, Screenlife, and What's Left to Shock
segments.note: on a culture that can stream a real death on the news

films.title: The Blair Witch Project
films.note: the found-footage template that felt genuinely new

films.title: Unfriended
films.note: screenlife horror's viable start

films.title: The Human Centipede
films.note: Perry's read: all concept, no follow-through. A movie that's over once you know the title

films.title: Kill List
films.note: cited for its tone rather than any single shock

films.title: Redacted
films.note: Brian De Palma's Iraq War film: Perry's example of footage too close to real atrocity to work as entertainment

films.title: The Card Counter
films.note: cited as a rare recent film that uses real American torture as backstory without turning it into spectacle

films.title: Shut In
films.note: a new Daily Wire film starring Vincent Gallo (directed by D.J. Caruso)

films.title: Buffalo 66
films.note: an earlier Gallo film, invoked as an 'indisputable masterpiece'

films.title: The Brown Bunny
films.note: the other Gallo masterpiece namedropped in passing

films.title: Irreversible
films.note: Gaspar Noé, described as scandalous on release and an institution now

films.title: Baise-moi
films.note: the French extreme sex-violence film that hit around the same time as 'Irreversible'

films.title: Breaking the Waves
films.note: cited as the tender side of Lars von Trier, against the deliberately upsetting side

films.title: Funny Games
films.note: Michael Haneke

films.title: Parasite
films.note: the case study for how little most audiences see of the wider world of foreign film

films.title: Martyrs
films.note: unheralded on release, now regarded by horror fans as the film that goes furthest of anything from its decade

segments.heading: Honorable Mentions
segments.note: named before Perry got around to his official five

films.title: Deep Red
films.note: Argento, the movie that first opened Perry's mind to this whole world

films.title: Possession
films.note: one of Perry's 10 favorite films ever, left off the ranked list because a recent restoration made it too accessible to still count as disreputable

segments.heading: Alex Ross Perry's Most Disreputable Movies
segments.subject: extreme, disreputable cinema
segments.note: ranked five to one, reserved for films whose reputation still scares people off

films.title: Deranged
films.note: No. 1: the real Ed Gein film. Lower-key and therefore more insane than its more famous cousins, with a cannibalism angle 'Texas Chainsaw' only implies.

films.title: Der Fan
films.note: No. 2: a German film about a girl warped by obsession with a pop star. Still shocking, Perry says, despite great music.

films.title: Cannibal Holocaust
films.note: No. 3: the basic pick, and still the one that made Perry's wife physically ill on a rewatch decades later.

films.title: Who Can Kill a Child?
films.note: No. 4: an unseen-by-Fennessey Spanish film about murderous children, ending on the same button as 'Hostel Part II.'

films.title: Pieces
films.note: No. 5: a Spanish slasher whose tagline is 'you don't have to go to Texas to have a chainsaw massacre.'
